current_implemented_build: 20260602-1730 Layer 1 v2 Asset Integration and Great Map Doctrine Intake
patch_type: Layer 1 v2 asset integration and Great Map doctrine intake
release_chain_status: 20260602-1730 is the current implemented build after V60.3.30. It adds code support for the new same-size black-background blue-white Layer 1 v2 assets, keeps legacy fallbacks, and captures Great Map doctrine for a future design pass.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs.
asset_rule: Layer 1 v2 assets are preferred only when available at assets/layer1-v2/. Legacy PNG files remain fallbacks until browser QA confirms the v2 files.

great_map_doctrine.summary: CyberShield should evolve from a platform that contains a TrustMap into a platform that behaves like a map.
great_map_doctrine.status: doctrine intake for future design pass

runtime_import_rule: The executive shell loads first. The full TrustMap loads when requested.
performance_doctrine: Load the full TrustMap only when requested.
Warm TrustMap images after the shell is usable.
Prefer asset optimization over more runtime patches when images remain slow.
controlling_doctrine: No score without a model. No model without evidence. No evidence without traceability. No action without consequence.
prototype_boundary: The current GitHub Pages build is a lightweight static advisory prototype.
qa_priority: Verify initial app shell remains fast on phone
Open TrustMap and confirm no broken image icons appear before v2 binaries are uploaded
Upload the seven v2 PNG files to assets/layer1-v2/
Confirm v2 assets replace legacy assets
Confirm all seven Layer 1 assets have the same apparent size
Confirm no new top-level tab exists
